sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a species of gazelle, so called from its leaping and bounding; from the root דּוּשׁ pr. to tread, but prob. also i.q. דּוּץ to leap, whence דִּיצָא, ܕܝܰܨܐܳ wild goat, gazelle (comp. Bochart, Hieroz. ii. page 270 and Rosenm. on the place), Deu 14:5. LXX. πύγαργος, Syr. and Targ. ܪܝܺܡ, both the Arab. الاروى, all of which words denote a kind of gazelle.

(2) [Dishon], pr.n.-(a) of a son of Seir, also the name of a district in Edom, so called from him. Gen 36:21, 30 Gen 36:30 1Ch 1:38.-(b) a grandson of Seir, Gen 36:25 1Ch 1:4.

[דִּישָׁן pr.n. m. Dishan, Gen 36:21, etc.]
